Hey guys and welcome back to the workbook walkthrough for the 67 golden questions to fuel Your funnel and I use that word pretty intentionally because this workbook goes beyond just getting the insights for your sales page It really is for that full spectrum of everything you're going to have to write for a particularly campaign or a product launch or sales process for any new Product so the initial iteration of this document actually came in 10x launches and the focus on that program is more related to using emails and Sequences to launch a product so this could be used for emails. This could be used for sales pages This could be used for every asset you are creating in a sales campaign So with that said you are going to want to invest a lot of energy up front here to really get those insights and data that you're Using across literally every part of your funnel. So if you are doing this on your own for your own product I do recommend about anywhere between two and four Sitdowns of an hour each to get this done if you're doing this with a client I recommend scheduling two to three 60 minute zoom calls You know on consecutive days, so you're really doing this deep dive quickly and keeping momentum going But you really do want to go through this and you want to call those little audibles when you have to you don't have to go in perfect sequence and you definitely want to leave room for flexibility so it doesn't just sound like you're You know running a questionnaire for an hour, which isn't fun for everybody So you do want to keep it fluid, but you also do want to use these questions in the sections of it as a guiding structure to make sure you're really getting the information you need to do an awesome job and Coach the conversion so with all that said First section is all about relevant Demographics and I use that word intentionally it has to be relevant demographics on their own are pretty Overrated in my opinion. They don't really provide a lot of value and insights instead you want to know what is Specifically at stake for that demographic. What is the consequence or how does the problem appear based on that demographic? So Gender and why that matters age and why that matters, you know putting this two together Male pattern baldness for a 23 year old single dude has much different stakes and consequences than male pattern baldness for a 60 year old grandfather number and age of children obviously Not getting enough sleep and having trouble sleeping at night and a solution to focus on that is gonna have different consequences for Somebody who doesn't have kids versus someone who does Income why this matters so an example of how that becomes relevant That could be a business tax accountant, you know the stakes of a business making Seven figures a year is a lot different than a business in its first year making 30 grand a year So obviously the stakes and consequences and getting the tax accounting right Matter and have very different consequences. So just to sum up relevant demographics If you are going to market based on demographic data, you have to be very clear and precise as to why that matters Next section is all about everyday Psychographics and I use that word every day Every day psychographics. This is where I Get that information and data to really bring my copy to life. So One of the questions I get asked a lot is precisely that like how do you have such colorful? Wively language in your headlines. Why are you confident taking those risks and being so specific about? I'm about to things I'm referencing in my copy and it all starts here on the exercises You're gonna do in section two and when you dial this in this is the difference between a boring headline like why aren't you making more sales and one that is all full of life Which is so why aren't you igniting your happy hormones with heaping doses of grass-fed butter rolling in PayPal notifications and blast Looping Beethoven's fifth through your Bose headphones. So one of them is White noise everyone's just gonna kind of look past that roll their eyes and the other one Just sings there's kind of this There's this quality to it where you can all of a sudden place That product within the context of your life not the other way around and that's what you want to do You want to place your product within the context of their life the life that they recognize not pull them into Your made-up reality of your product. So everyday Psychographics is where we build those connection points and we have a few questions here that can really Yeah, really open up Yeah, kind of open up the current so you can see what is going on in their life and reference those things wherever a person Appropriate in your headphone in your headlines not your headphones Sorry in your headlines and the rest of your copy. So what does their typical morning look like do they drive take an Uber? What are they listening to through all kind of headphones? What are they drinking is it a mug a thermos? What apps are they checking? What are they loading up Skype zoom Spotify iTunes while waiting for another Apple update? Whatever it is just be hyper specific about what their morning looks like and what are their sign failed moments? So if you're marketing to Writers who spend a lot of time at a Starbucks. What is that everyday little annoyance, you know for me? it's that Person who reserves I use the word person very loosely because there's a certain place in hell reserved for these humans but who reserved the last Available seat in the Starbucks with their bag before ordering and they're behind you in line So you got there first but they've already reserved the last table So like that is something that anyone who does their work out of a Starbucks could totally relate to mention it bring your Copy into their life bring your product into the context of their life What does their night routine look like what books shows media? Are they likely to be consuming right now? All these things just once again give you the insights and the data to really bring your copy to life throughout Charged up ethos graphics. So this is kind of similar, but it's used to stir up a little more emotion You know, where can you take a stand with them? Where can you invite your audience to take a stand and join you in a particular ethos or turn the other way if that just Doesn't resonate with them. So it's important to know what are they tired of seeing what makes them sick? What is it about the way you run your business or program that could resonate with their personal values? So this isn't the time to Hide and be generic and be vague. It's time to really kind of Take a put a line in the sand there, especially as it relates to your market and the thing you do you don't have to Start talking about beliefs that are irrelevant to the outcomes you're trying to produce for your client But where it does relate definitely be clear More questions to list at that How does your tribe inspire you to be better what you refuse to let them be exposed to or endure anymore? This is a really important one for bonding and bringing them closer In what other areas can you take stand for them? What suspicions? Conspiracies or fallacies can you confirm for them? That is a gift and a major trust-builder when they can see an expert or an authority on a topic confirming What they already thought and had a suspicion Was stacked against them so validating? Their fears and concerns around a certain topic is a huge opportunity to create affinity and trust And of course who are those common enemies call them out either by name or by? Convention it doesn't have to be a particular person. It could be a process a way of doing things a pattern a an ethos so a common enemy doesn't have to be human it could be Anything that is currently holding them back from achieving what they want to achieve or overcoming a certain pain Section 4 is about creating That intimacy and that transparency so these questions will definitely unlock certain anecdotes of the author or creator or Company that is selling the product And those are things you're going to be able to inject throughout the copy and just even create hooks and know what stories to tell So why do you do what you do? What is it about your audience's struggles that breaks your heart? Do you have a story to back that up? What was the last customer experience that really kind of shook you to your core? And you weren't able to take that next call because you had to recover from the one you had just experienced What reality do you see for them that they don't yet see for themselves? So this is more of a point of frustration like what makes you want to just like pull your client out from the screen and say you can Freakin do this like why don't you yet see it this way? So what is that reality that you see for them that they don't yet see themselves? What pains you most about their inability to see that potential and what was your biggest failure as a coach or educator or program creator? You know, how do you feel that you let the market down? So being very transparent about these things Doesn't diminish your credibility. It just creates transparency and some very valid humaneness around how you do things Moving on so we have a few more questions here on the Relationship to your avatar. What's the most gratifying or fulfilling experience you've had? How did that experience exceed your expectations and maybe open your eyes to a deeper impact? You didn't even know you were making so that makes for a really cool story or headline or email hook How deep is your commitment serving this market? I'm assuming it's pretty high but talk about that You know, how have you re-engineered your own life to create that impact? How have you gone all in? You know, how have you bled for that mission? What have you sacrificed or given up in the name of that mission either as far as business opportunities go or personal opportunities? Go what have you done to reinforce the fact that you are all in and serving this market in this way? And what have you learned from your market? So how have they inspired you be that? Creator who's not in that ivory tower and immune to being moved and inspired by your market But be with your tribe. How have they inspired you? and of course the big world ethos the world will be a better place when and That's going to open up a conversation and another point for your audience to nod their head and say yep I see things that way as well. I want to work with this person Section five. This is where we're really going to get into those sacred cows That's going to be a major part of your sales page. So these questions are all entry points to that What's the biggest excuse your avatar makes for having not achieved a favorable outcome yet? And how might that be justified? You know, how can you validate that excuse? Once again, that builds trust when you can validate their suspicions But of course you also have to answer. What is it about you or your method that over comes it? So that's going to play a point in the reframes How do your biggest competitors fall short and overcoming it? So here positioning against competitors through those sacred cows What is a false belief they have about the industry about themselves and their ability or capacity to achieve it? about the best way to solve the problem or achieve the outcome and Here's one of my favorites. Why might it be a good thing that they've waited until now? So this is a particularly useful question if What you're selling is in a fairly mature and saturated market and your customers are likely already Disillusioned, you know, they haven't had that result. Maybe they haven't even invested in anything significant Why is it a good thing? They've waited until now. How has the level of maturity of the industry or solutions? Made now the ideal moment So a lot of people feel bad about waiting on the sidelines if you could talk about why that was a good thing that just ramps up their ability and capacity and willingness to take action now Yep getting concrete listing three to five of the biggest objections They might have around forging ahead and for each explore how you can create more comfort and ease and then getting very clear on Those sacred cows so these are those commonly held and widely accepted beliefs and paradigms that don't Serve your prospect meaning they're the ones that they'd be most ready and willing to build to be Release from if someone in a place of authority and experience was there to grant them that release So that's the gift we want to give them with these sacred cows We also want to give them opportunities of exclusive empowerment and self forgiveness so everyone wants to cash in on their Privilege points on things they've experienced and achieved in the past that Exclusively empower them to work towards this result No one wants what everyone else can have they want what they are exclusively empowered to pursue Themselves and the same goes not only for accomplishments, but missteps regrets and sunk cost So this is where you give them the gift of channeling all those things and using them as the building blocks For what they want to achieve, which is very very powerful I've mentioned it before but check out the Markets search for meaning template in six-figure emails for more information on how to leverage that And of course how can those previous failures be reframed as necessary steps or prerequisites for where they are now So this is all about market search for meaning the whole Take on on the Victor Frankel Reframing and logo therapy here. How can you take all those things and make them necessary steps and prerequisites? really awesome stuff neck brace moments these are all about Getting this Agreement throughout the more you can have them nodding their head and feeling this sense of wow you really get me That's how you build that trust that you can eventually deliver the results. So you do want to get it Throughout not only in the final stages of your sales page, but any chance you get so where can you confirm to them about? Their present experience or struggle as it relates to that problem, you know, what can you confirm to them about why solving this really matters in their life? So here you're just really Validating what's already happening in their head. You want to create that head nod Moment every opportunity you can get before making that ask What can confirm to them about their skepticism and reasons for being rightfully skeptical and where have they been most let down or disillusioned? Probably the most powerful place to get agreement and head nodding before making your actual offer Section 8 this is all about Your product it's gonna weigh in heavily in the product tour aspect of your sales page So what is the main magic one moment or outcome your product can deliver? Why is that important to your prospect and going deeper? Why does that matter going even deeper? Why does that last one matter? Often you'll arrive to this point in number five where you are identifying the opportunities That are now available to them at the end point of your product and this is Exciting to many people they want to be in a position to To pursue new and exciting opportunities that weren't available to them before so what is it about the end point that your product or solution? Takes them that now enables them and empowers them to explore these new things that have been reserved for one day status And what other intangible or unexpected problems does buying your product solve? You know for a lot of people there's a lot of relief and just freeing them from the search For a solution or confidence to take all this energy and motivation They have to solving that problem and actually doubling down on it knowing they're gonna be getting that return on effort and Then I like doing this exercise here where we just attack the four level of benefits They'll be experiencing so if you can talk about something immediate and tangible immediate and emotional long-term and tangible and Long-term emotional that's just a really effective way of categorizing the different types of benefits that your product will deliver section 9 this will Work itself into your email copy and your sales page. I love using this throughout pretty much anywhere I can it's those divergent roads and parallel realities where you're contrasting the two different possibilities for them you're really framing this as a Choice, you know, they are choosing reality a or reality B and Contrasting what they could look like in three weeks three months three days and 12 months from now both with and without your product and that actually creates a very Very vivid and all-encompassing Comparison of the two different potentialities in front of them. So this could work really well in that risk Mitigator close and then finally we have all the advanced empathy exercises that I'm not gonna go through now We do have another Supplemental workbook for you to go deeper on to those But we do have a few bonus questions on the moment of highest tension that I want to go through here. So this is Powerful stuff that you can really reflect back to them, you know, where are they judging themselves? most Critically and just calling that out you offer a ton of relief, you know, that is a service That is a value you give to your audience when you can point that out and relieve them from it What can't they accept or tolerate in themselves? I will reveal that moment of highest tension. What would they be most embarrassed of if their peers saw or knew? So really getting into the hidden aspects of that moment of highest tension that stuff that is just so cringe worthy that That it's more of their inner experience that you're reflecting back So another entry point into that is this question here and where have they failed to meet their own? Expectations or the expectations of others we put so much pressure on ourselves to meet our own expectations That that is often a source of that moment of highest tension at least the inner the one the one lived Between our ears So the more of this you can really channel and reflect back the more you're gonna have this Divided makeup of that moment of highest tension of both the outer experience of it and the inner experience of it Which just creates this entire? section where they feel you have perfectly Captured every dimension of the problem in its most acute and pressing form so a Lot of stuff there. I do recommend you go through this at a pace that works for you You can Relisten to this as many times as you need just pausing between every question But I do encourage you to do it and not skip this work as it really is going to unlock the insights You need to coach that conversion. So that's it for this video I'll see you in the next one where we go deeper into those advanced visualizations
